description: Helping/Hurting Factor Engine & Dynamic Pillar Statuses — replace mock data with real factor generation

userStories.title: Create Factor data model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Factor interface so factors have a consistent shape across the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/models/Factor.ts with Factor interface: id (string), type ('helping' | 'hurting'), pillar (Pillar), name (string), explanation (string), expandedContent (string | undefined), actionLabel (string | undefined), actionType ('pearl_pro' | 'rebate' | 'improvement' | 'manual' | undefined), source ('reading' | 'equipment' | 'symptom' | 'onboarding'), severity ('info' | 'attention' | 'action')
Export Factor type from src/models/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create FactorEngine service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pure service that generates helping/hurting factors from device readings, equipment, symptoms, and onboarding answers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/services/FactorEngine.ts
Export generateFactorsFromReadings(readings: DeviceReading[]): Factor[] — good readings = helping, bad = hurting using SCORE thresholds
Export generateFactorsFromEquipment(equipment: EquipmentItem[]): Factor[] — good = helping, aging = hurting (attention), replaceSoon = hurting (action)
Export generateFactorsFromSymptoms(symptoms: LoggedSymptom[]): Factor[] — each symptom creates hurting factor per affected pillar
Export generateFactorsFromOnboarding(answers: OnboardingAnswers): Factor[] — good = helping, bad/some = hurting
Export generateAllFactors(params): Factor[] combining all 4 generators
Export derivePillarStatus(factors, pillar): PillarStatus — action-severity hurting = needsWork, attention = typical, else good
Export derivePillarStatuses(factors): Record<Pillar, PillarStatus> for all 5 SCORE pillars
All functions are pure (no side effects, no async)
Export from src/services/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire PillarDetailScreen to use FactorEngine
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see real helping/hurting factors on the pillar detail screen based on my actual home data.
acceptanceCriteria: PillarDetailScreen imports useDevices, useEquipment, useSymptoms, useOnboarding from context
PillarDetailScreen imports generateAllFactors, derivePillarStatus from services
Remove PILLAR_DATA mock constant and local PillarInfo, FactorData, EquipmentData interfaces
Add PILLAR_INFO map with 5 canonical SCORE pillar IDs (safety/comfort/operations/resilience/energy) containing name, icon, explanation
Factors generated via generateAllFactors() with context data, filtered by pillarId
Pillar status derived via derivePillarStatus(factors, pillarId)
Helping/hurting sections render from filtered factors
Equipment section uses real equipment from EquipmentContext filtered by type-to-pillar mapping
If no factors for pillar, show existing empty state
FactorCard onAction calls onTakeAction prop
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire MyHomeScreen to derive pillar statuses dynamically
userStories.description: As a user, I want the My Home tab pillar cards to reflect my actual home performance.
acceptanceCriteria: MyHomeScreen imports useDevices, useSymptoms, useOnboarding from context (useEquipment already imported)
MyHomeScreen imports generateAllFactors, derivePillarStatuses from services
Remove hardcoded status from PILLARS array — compute dynamically
Compute factors and derive statuses via derivePillarStatuses(factors)
Each PillarCard receives its dynamic status
Default status is good when no data sources available
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire HomeScreen to derive pillar statuses dynamically
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Home tab pillar summary to reflect my actual home performance.
acceptanceCriteria: HomeScreen imports useEquipment from context (useDevices, useSymptoms, useOnboarding already imported)
HomeScreen imports generateAllFactors, derivePillarStatuses from services
Remove hardcoded status from PILLARS array — compute dynamically
Replace existing derivePillarStatuses() local function with FactorEngine version
DailySummaryCard receives pillar statuses mapped: PillarStatus good→ReadingStatus good, typical→attention, needsWork→action
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Connect hurting factor actions to navigation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to tap action buttons on hurting factors and be taken to relevant screens.
acceptanceCriteria: PillarDetailScreenWrapper in RootNavigator passes onTakeAction that navigates based on factor ID
FactorEngine assigns actionType to factors: equipment replaceSoon → rebate, equipment aging → pearl_pro, symptom → improvement, reading action → pearl_pro
onTakeAction navigates: pearl_pro → PearlPros, rebate → RebateDetail (rebate-1), improvement → Main (Improve tab)
Typecheck passes
